name: Park State Bank & Trust
description_short: Park State Bank & Trust is a locally-owned community bank based in Woodland Park offering conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, and jumbo mortgages with personalized underwriting and responsive service.
description_long:
Park State Bank & Trust is a locally-owned community bank headquartered in Woodland Park, Colorado, serving individuals, families, businesses, and communities in their region. The bank emphasizes local decision-making, community investment, and relationship-based banking rather than a purely transactional model. While they offer full banking services (checking, savings, online banking, ATM access), their mortgage division is a significant service line with dedicated loan officers specializing in multiple loan products.

Their mortgage services include conventional conforming loans, FHA mortgages, VA loans for veterans, USDA Rural Development financing, HECM reverse mortgages, jumbo loans for higher-value properties, and their proprietary Portfolio 5/30 product designed for borrowers who don't qualify for conventional or government mortgages. They employ NMLS-licensed loan officers (Jack Galsterer for construction loans and Craig Spivey for general mortgage lending) who provide pre-qualification, rate shopping, and refinancing guidance. Their stated approach emphasizes responsive timelines, personalized underwriting, and willingness to work through complex loan scenarios beyond standardized criteria.

Park State Bank distinguishes itself through several community-focused practices: locally-made lending decisions, deep knowledge of their regional market, flexible underwriting that considers individual circumstances alongside credit metrics, and significant community investment through donations and sponsorships. They position themselves against the "spaceship style" approach of larger lenders, claiming higher success rates on difficult loans and a focus on quality service over loan volume. Their Portfolio 5/30 product specifically addresses borrowers with non-traditional profiles or temporary financing needs.

A realistic assessment notes this is a regional community bank limited to their service area, not a nationwide lender. Their mortgage offerings are standard industry products with the addition of their 5/30 portfolio option. The website lacks specific rate information, APR ranges, or loan terms, requiring direct contact for pricing. Their emphasis on "flexible" and individualized underwriting is positive but means approval likelihood depends heavily on local relationships and circumstances rather than transparent automated criteria.
services: Conventional conforming mortgages with competitive rates
FHA mortgages with process navigation support
VA loans for veterans with emphasis on maximizing service benefits
HECM reverse mortgages with education and guidance
Portfolio 5/30 loans (5-year balloon, 30-year amortization) for non-qualifying borrowers
Construction loans (specialized officer: Jack Galsterer, NMLS #2230404)
ATM access with surcharge-free locator tool
pros: NMLS-licensed mortgage officers (Jack Galsterer #2230404, Craig Spivey #2035040) with specialized expertise in construction and conventional lending
Portfolio 5/30 loans bridge gap for non-qualifying borrowers with streamlined local underwriting and flexible terms
Offers full spectrum of mortgage products: conventional, FHA, VA, USDA Rural Development, HECM reverse, and jumbo loans
Responsive pre-qualification and rate-shopping service with daily market monitoring, not just loan volume focus
All lending decisions made locally by staff with deep community knowledge and individual loan circumstance review
Willingness to navigate complex/non-standard loan scenarios, not just 'easy loans'
Established community bank with community investment and locally-sourced deposits
cons: Regional community bank limited to service area—not available nationwide; geography-dependent access
Website provides no specific mortgage rates, APR ranges, loan terms, or pricing transparency—requires calling or applying to learn rates
Flexible underwriting based on local relationships and individual assessment means approval likelihood not standardized or predictable
No online mortgage application portal evident; process appears to require direct contact with loan officers
Loan approval speed/timelines not specified beyond general claim of responsiveness

contact.address: 710 W Us Highway 24, Woodland Park, CO 80863

best_for: Colorado residents in Woodland Park and surrounding communities seeking personalized mortgage service with local decision-making
Veterans and active military using VA loan benefits who value relationship-based lending over impersonal processing
Borrowers in rural areas who qualify for USDA Rural Development programs
Non-traditional borrowers unable to meet conventional loan standards but needing mortgage financing (Portfolio 5/30 product)
diagnosis: Park State Bank & Trust is best for borrowers in Colorado's Woodland Park region who prioritize relationship-based, locally-made lending decisions and are willing to work directly with loan officers rather than use online-only lenders. The main caveat is that this is a regional community bank with no transparent online pricing—you must call or visit to learn rates and terms, and approval depends on local underwriting discretion rather than automated algorithms, making it less suitable for borrowers seeking quick, standardized, nationwide mortgage shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 710 W Us Highway 24, Woodland Park, CO 80863
